name: STUMPY (Time Series Matrix Profile)

performance.notes: Numba JIT + multicore/GPU; STUMPED for distributed; fast STOMP algorithm

agent_readiness.notes: Matrix Profile for time series motif discovery and anomaly detection. import stumpy. import numpy as np. mp = stumpy.stump(T, m=50). mp[:, 0] → matrix profile distances. mp[:, 1] → matrix profile indices (nearest neighbor). motif_idx = np.argsort(mp[:, 0])[0]. Discord (anomaly): discord_idx = np.argsort(mp[:, 0])[::-1][0]. Streaming: stumpy.stumpi(T, m, egress=False). Multidimensional: stumpy.mstump(T, m). Subsequence similarity join: stumpy.stump(T_A, m, T_B). Chain discovery: stumpy.allc(mp). GPU: stumpy.gpu_stump. Dask distributed: stumpy.stumped.

gotchas: Window size m must be chosen carefully — too small misses patterns, too large is slow
Matrix profile is O(n^2) computation — optimized but still slow for very long series
Numba JIT compilation adds 30-60s startup on first run
Multidimensional matrix profile (mstump) interpretation requires domain knowledge
best_when: Time series motif discovery: finding repeated patterns in sensor data
Time series similarity join and segmentation
When distance-based time series analysis outperforms model-based approaches
avoid_when: Short time series (< 200 points) where simpler correlation suffices
When interpretable models are needed (STUMPY is non-parametric)
summary: STUMPY computes Matrix Profile for time series motif/anomaly discovery. stumpy.stump(T, m=50) → (distances, indices). motif_idx = argmin(mp[:,0]). discord_idx = argmax(mp[:,0]). Streaming: stumpi(). GPU: gpu_stump(). Dask: stumped(). BSD-3 by TD Ameritrade. Best for motif discovery and anomaly detection in long sensor/IoT time series. Numba 30-60s cold start. O(n^2) computation. Window size m requires tuning.
